threadTitle: Things that instantly reveal you just moved here
body: You still think 'the Tube' is just the Underground. Real locals call the Overground the 'Coffee Line' for all the cafés along the route.
Saw a guy on the N381 loudly arguing with a lamp post last night, thought it was broken until I realized he was trying to convince it he was right.

threadTitle: When you knew you were actually living here
body: The day I saw a tango lesson in the park next to my building and everyone just joined in, like it was the most normal thing ever.

threadTitle: Things Berlin does that nowhere else does
body: You can grab a beer at 8 am in Friedrichshain and nobody bats an eye. It's just another day at a Kiosk, living that Berliner freedom.

threadTitle: Real signs someone is from here vs just working here
body: Only locals know to avoid the Civic Center BART station after a loud bang – the tourists are all still waiting for their rides.

threadTitle: Muni moments that define this city
body: Caught the 21 this morning, and a saxophonist hopped on, turned the whole bus into a mini concert. Almost forgot we were all late to work.

threadTitle: Things tourists get wrong about living here
body: Tourists often think Gràcia is just for hipsters, but many families have lived here for generations, preserving hidden gardens and local bakeries.

threadTitle: How to spot a recent arrival
body: You can tell by the way they’re still using Google Maps to navigate the streets instead of just following the sun.

threadTitle: Things that instantly reveal you just moved here
body: You still call it 'the tube' instead of just 'the overground' when you're in Hackney. You'll learn soon enough.
Last Friday, a woman on the 55 recited Shakespeare to the driver. He humored her with a dramatic bow at Stockwell.

threadTitle: When you knew you were actually living here
body: When I spotted a barista in a local café confidently repairing the espresso machine while chatting about the latest BVG strike. That’s Neukölln for you.

threadTitle: Things Berlin does that nowhere else does
body: Only in Kreuzberg do you see the spontaneous techno parties popping up in courtyard gardens, complete with fairy lights and eclectic crowds. It’s a vibe like no other.

threadTitle: Real signs someone is from here vs just working here
body: You know someone’s a local when they can spot the difference between a thrift store and a high-end resale shop—instantly, without hesitation.

threadTitle: Muni moments that define this city
body: Caught a Muni delay at Civic Center because a dog decided to take a nap on the tracks. Only in SF would that be a normal Tuesday afternoon.

threadTitle: Things tourists get wrong about living here
body: Tourists think we all drink vermut daily, but most of us save it for Sundays in the square, surrounded by old friends and gossip.

threadTitle: How to spot a recent arrival
body: They’re the ones standing on the corner, staring at the map, looking more confused than a cat in a dog park.
